subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: f(x) = ln(x)/x — Tangent Line at e², Critical Point, Inflection Point, Limit as x→0⁺
stimulus: Let $f$ be the function given by $f(x) = \dfrac{\ln x}{x}$ for all $x > 0$. The derivative of $f$ is given by $f'(x) = \dfrac{1 - \ln x}{x^2}$.

parts.prompt: Write an equation for the line tangent to the graph of $f$ at $x = e^2$.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $f(e^2) = 2/e^2$, $f'(e^2) = -1/e^4$. Tangent line: $y = \frac{2}{e^2} - \frac{1}{e^4}(x - e^2)$.

parts.prompt: Find the $x$-coordinate of the critical point of $f$. Determine whether this point is a relative minimum, a relative maximum, or neither for the function $f$. Justify your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $f'(x) = 0$ when $1 - \ln x = 0$, so $x = e$. This is a relative maximum because $f'$ changes from positive to negative at $x = e$.

scoring_points.description: Correct classification: relative maximum at $x = e$

required_elements: Correct classification: relative maximum at $x = e$

alternatives.correct_example: $f'(x)$ changes from positive to negative at $x = e$ (i.e., $f'(x) > 0$ for $0 < x < e$ and $f'(x) < 0$ for $x > e$)

parts.prompt: The graph of the function $f$ has exactly one point of inflection. Find the $x$-coordinate of this point.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $f''(x) = (-3 + 2\ln x)/x^3$. $f''(x) = 0$ when $2\ln x = 3$, so $x = e^{3/2}$. $f''$ changes sign at $x = e^{3/2}$, confirming an inflection point.

alternatives.correct_example: $-3 + 2\ln x = 0 \Rightarrow x = e^{3/2}$; this is an inflection point because $f''$ changes sign at $x = e^{3/2}$

parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: As $x \to 0^+$, $\ln x \to -\infty$ and $x \to 0^+$, so $\ln x / x \to -\infty$. Answer $-\infty$ or DNE are both accepted.
